devanagari: रामकृष्णयोर्मथुरायां प्रवेशः रजकवधः वायकमालाकारयोरनुग्रहेणं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । स्तुवतस्तस्य भगवान् दर्शयित्वा जले वपुः । भूयः समाहरत् क...
english_devnagari: rāmakṛṣṇayormathurāyāṃ praveśaḥ rajakavadhaḥ vāyakamālākārayoranugraheṇaṃ ca śrīśuka uvāca stuvatastasya bhagavān darśayitvā jale vapuḥ bhūyaḥ samāharat kṛṣṇo naṭo nāṭyamivātmanaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having manifested his (original Viṣṇu) form in the water to Akrūra, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa withdrew it again, even while he was glorifying him,[1] like an actor (who instantly retires) after playing his role.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Despite Akrūra’s continuation of praise. This shows Kṛṣṇa’s indifference to praise (anādare ṣaṣṭḥī)—VT

devanagari: सोऽपि चान्तर्हितं वीक्ष्य जलाद् उन्मज्ज्य सत्वरः । कृत्वा चावश्यकं सर्वं विस्मितो रथमागमत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: so kṛtvā cāvaśyakaṃ sarvaṃ vismito rathamāgamat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Noticing that the vision had disappeared, Akrūra emerged from water. He hastily performed the (mid-day) religious duties. He then returned to the chariot still wondering at the vision.

devanagari: तं अपृच्छद् हृषीकेशः किं ते दृष्टमिवाद्‍भुतम् । भूमौ वियति तोये वा तथा त्वां लक्षयामहे ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ apṛcchad hṛṣīkeśaḥ kiṃ te dṛṣṭamivād‍bhutam bhūmau viyati toye vā tathā tvāṃ lakṣayāmahe
translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa (the Controller of Senses) enquired, “have you seen anything miraculous either on the land or in the water or in the sky? I surmise (from your appearance) that you have perceived some such wonder.

devanagari: श्रीअक्रूर उवाच । अद्‍भुतानीह यावन्ति भूमौ वियति वा जले । त्वयि विश्वात्मके तानि किं मेऽदृष्टं विपश्यतः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīakrūra uvāca ad‍bhutānīha yāvanti bhūmau viyati vā jale tvayi viśvātmake tāni kiṃ me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Akrura replied:
“Whatever wonders are there on the earth, in the sky or in the water in this world exist in you who constitute the whole of the universe. What marvel can remain un-witnesseḍ by me who have a direct vision of you?

devanagari: यत्राद्‍भुतानि सर्वाणि भूमौ वियति वा जले । तं त्वानुपश्यतो ब्रह्मन् किं मे दृष्टमिहाद्‍भुतम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yatrād‍bhutāni sarvāṇi bhūmau viyati vā jale taṃ tvānupaśyato brahman kiṃ me dṛṣṭamihād‍bhutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ śā class 4 verb], [ablative single from √ śā class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ śā class 4 verb]
[ablative single from √ śā class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ śā class 4 verb]

translation: Oh Supreme Lord! When I directly perceive you who accommodate all the marvels within you, what wonder, either on the earth, in the water or the sky could be witnessed by me.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा चोदयामास स्यन्दनं गान्दिनीसुतः । मथुरां अनयद् रामं कृष्णं चैव दिनात्यये ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā codayāmāsa syandanaṃ gāndinīsutaḥ mathurāṃ anayad rāmaṃ kṛṣṇaṃ caiva dinātyaye
translation: Replying him in these words, Akrūra, the son of Gāndinī, drove the chariot and at the end of the day, he brought Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa to Mathurā,

devanagari: मार्गे ग्रामजना राजन् तत्र तत्रोपसङ्गताः । वसुदेवसुतौ वीक्ष्य प्रीता दृष्टिं न चाददुः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: mārge grāmajanā rājan tatra tatropasaṅgatāḥ vasudevasutau vīkṣya prītā dṛṣṭiṃ na cādaduḥ

translation: People in the country-side who assembled at various stages on the way, became delighted to see the sons of Vasudcva (Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa) and were not able to take away their eyes from them.

devanagari: तावद् व्रजौकसस्तत्र नन्दगोपादयोऽग्रतः । पुरोपवनमासाद्य प्रतीक्षन्तोऽवतस्थिरे ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvad vrajaukasastatra nandagopādayo puropavanamāsādya pratīkṣanto
translation: In the meanwhile, Nanda, the cowherd, and other inhabitants of Vraja who came ahead of them (by a short-cut) stood waiting for them in a park near the city (Mathurā).

devanagari: तान् समेत्याह भगवान् अक्रूरं जगदीश्वरः । गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणिं प्रश्रितं प्रहसन्निव ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tān sametyāha bhagavān akrūraṃ jagadīśvaraḥ gṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇiṃ praśritaṃ prahasanniva
translation: After joining them (Nanda and his group of cowherds) the glorious Lord of the world took Akrūra by hand while Akrūra was modestly bowing, and spoke to him as if with a laugh.

devanagari: भवान् प्रविशतामग्रे सहयानः पुरीं गृहम् । वयं त्विहावमुच्याथ ततो द्रक्ष्यामहे पुरीम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: bhavān praviśatāmagre sahayānaḥ purīṃ gṛham vayaṃ tvihāvamucyātha tato drakṣyāmahe purīm
translation: “You may go in advance with the chariot to the city and return home. We shall get down here and after resting for some time, shall go to the city.”

devanagari: श्रीअक्रूर उवाच । नाहं भवद्‍भ्यां रहितः प्रवेक्ष्ये मथुरां प्रभो । त्यक्तुं नार्हसि मां नाथ भक्तं ते भक्तवत्सल ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīakrūra uvāca nāhaṃ bhavad‍bhyāṃ rahitaḥ pravekṣye mathurāṃ prabho tyaktuṃ nārhasi māṃ nātha bhaktaṃ te bhaktavatsala

[nominative dual from √ īkṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ īkṣ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ īkṣ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ īkṣ class 1 verb]

translation:
Akrūra requested:
Oh Lord! I shall not enter Mathurā leaving you both here. It does not behove you, Oh Protector (of the world) who are so kind to your devotees, to forsake me who am your votary.

devanagari: आगच्छ याम गेहान् नः सनाथान् कुर्वधोक्षज । सहाग्रजः सगोपालैः सुहृद्भिश्च सुहृत्तम ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: āgaccha yāma gehān naḥ sanāthān kurvadhokṣaja sahāgrajaḥ sagopālaiḥ suhṛdbhiśca suhṛttama
translation: Be pleased to come. Let us go together. Oh Lord! You transcend sense-perception. Oh best of friends! Visiting our house along with your elder brother (Balarāma), with your friends and cowherds bless our home with your august presence.

devanagari: पुनीहि पादरजसा गृहान्नो गृहमेधिनाम् । यच्छौचेनानुतृप्यन्ति पितरः साग्नयः सुराः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: punīhi pādarajasā gṛhānno gṛhamedhinām yacchaucenānutṛpyanti pitaraḥ sāgnayaḥ surāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ tṛp class 4 verb]

translation: Be gracious to sanctify our houses with the dust of your feet as we are after all householders (inevitably committing five types of sins[2]). For the ancestors and gods including the Fire-god get satisfied and propitiated with the water used for washing your feet (which is kept in a vessel or small reservoir in the courtyard of the house).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Manu enumerates five types of inevitable sins caused in the household work such as ‘cooking on a stove’, ‘pounding’, ‘crushing’, ‘brushing up of vessel’ etc. as follows:
pañca sūnā gṛhasthasya cullī peṣaṇyupaskarāḥ /
kaṇḍanī coda-kumbhaśca-manu 3.66

devanagari: अवनिज्याङ्घ्रियुगलं आसीत् श्लोक्यो बलिर्महान् । ऐश्वर्यं अतुलं लेभे गतिं चैकान्तिनां तु या ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: avanijyāṅghriyugalaṃ āsīt ślokyo balirmahān aiśvaryaṃ atulaṃ lebhe gatiṃ caikāntināṃ tu yā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: By washing the pair of your feet the great king Bali attained sanctifying fame, incomparable affluence and supreme sovereignty) as also the goal reached by absolute votaries.

devanagari: आपस्तेऽङ्घ्र्यवनेजन्यः त्रींल्लोकान् शुचयोऽपुनन् । शिरसाधत्त याः शर्वः स्वर्याताः सगरात्मजाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: āpaste śirasādhatta yāḥ śarvaḥ svaryātāḥ sagarātmajāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: The sacred waters (viz. the river Gaṅgā) that washed your feet have sanctified the three worlds, were borne by god Śiva on his head and by contact with which the sons of Sagara attained to the heaven.

devanagari: देवदेव जगन्नाथ पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तन । यदूत्तमोत्तमःश्लोक नारायण नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: devadeva jagannātha puṇyaśravaṇakīrtana yadūttamottamaḥśloka nārāyaṇa namo
translation: I bow to you, Oh God of gods, Oh Lord of the universe, the listening and recital of whose glory bring spiritual merit; On the foremost one in the Yadu clan, Oh Nārāyaṇa of glorious reputation.”

devanagari: श्रीभगवनुवाच । आयास्ये भवतो गेहं अहमार्यसमन्वितः । यदुचक्रद्रुहं हत्वा वितरिष्ये सुहृत्प्रियम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavanuvāca āyāsye bhavato gehaṃ ahamāryasamanvitaḥ yaducakradruhaṃ hatvā vitariṣye suhṛtpriyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The glorious Lord replied:
It is after slaying Kaṃsa who is the enemy of our Yadu clan, that I shall visit your house in company of my elder brother (Balarāma) and shall bring delight to my friends.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवमुक्तो भगवता सोऽक्रूरो विमना इव । पुरीं प्रविष्टः कंसाय कर्मावेद्य गृहं ययौ ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evamukto bhagavatā so purīṃ praviṣṭaḥ kaṃsāya karmāvedya gṛhaṃ yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Akrūra who was thus replied, became rather disappointed. He, however, entered the city, reported (to Kaṃsa) the account of the mission assigned to him and returned home.

devanagari: अथापराह्ने भगवान् कृष्णः सङ्कर्षणान्वितः । मथुरां प्राविशद् गोपैः दिदृक्षुः परिवारितः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: athāparāhne bhagavān kṛṣṇaḥ saṅkarṣaṇānvitaḥ mathurāṃ prāviśad gopaiḥ didṛkṣuḥ parivāritaḥ
translation: The next day in the afternoon, Lord Kṛṣṇa, accompanied by Balarāma and surrounded by the cowherds, entered Mathura with a view to see the city.

devanagari: ददर्श तां स्फाटिकतुङ्ग गोपुर । द्वारां बृहद् हेमकपाटतोरणाम् । ताम्रारकोष्ठां परिखादुरासदां । उद्यान रम्योप वनोपशोभिताम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: dadarśa tāṃ sphāṭikatuṅga gopura dvārāṃ bṛhad hemakapāṭatoraṇām tāmrārakoṣṭhāṃ parikhādurāsadāṃ udyāna ramyopa vanopaśobhitām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[vocative single from √ ram class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ram ]
[vocative single from √ ram class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ram ]
[nominative single from √ ram class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ram ]

translation: He saw the city Mathurā with high gate-ways and entrances made of crystals, with huge doors of gold beautified with gold arches. The granaries thereof were made of copper and brass. It was inaccessible due to deep moats around it. Its outskirts were made attractive with parks and beautiful gardens.

devanagari: सौवर्णशृङ्गाटकहर्म्यनिष्कुटैः । श्रेणीसभाभिः भवनैरुपस्कृताम् । वैदूर्यवज्रामलनीलविद्रुमैः । मुक्ताहरिद्भिर्वलभीषु वेदिषु ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: sauvarṇaśṛṅgāṭakaharmyaniṣkuṭaiḥ śreṇīsabhābhiḥ bhavanairupaskṛtām vaidūryavajrāmalanīlavidrumaiḥ muktāharidbhirvalabhīṣu vediṣu
translation: It appeared beautiful with its quadrangles (where four roads meet) decorated with gold and with mansions of gold with suitable pleasure gardens attached to each of them. It was decorated with guild-halls of different classes of artisans and with buildings of other citizens. It was filled with the joyful cooing of pigeons and joyous notes of the peacocks perching on the window-grills and platforms, eaves (awnings) and seats (under trees) all inlaid with Vaiḍūrya (cat’s eyes) diamonds, crystals, sapphires, corals, pearls and emeralds. Its streets, market places and quadrangles were profusely sprinkled with water and were strewn with flowers, sprouts, parched paddy and unbroken rice-grains.

devanagari: जुष्टेषु जालामुखरन्ध्रकुट्टिमेषु । आविष्टपारावतबर्हिनादिताम् । संसिक्तरथ्यापणमार्गचत्वरां । प्रकीर्णमाल्याङ्कुरलाजतण्डुलाम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: juṣṭeṣu jālāmukharandhrakuṭṭimeṣu āviṣṭapārāvatabarhināditām saṃsiktarathyāpaṇamārgacatvarāṃ prakīrṇamālyāṅkuralājataṇḍulām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative plural from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [locative plural from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[locative plural from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [locative plural from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: It appeared beautiful with its quadrangles (where four roads meet) decorated with gold and with mansions of gold with suitable pleasure gardens attached to each of them. It was decorated with guild-halls of different classes of artisans and with buildings of other citizens. It was filled with the joyful cooing of pigeons and joyous notes of the peacocks perching on the window-grills and platforms, eaves (awnings) and seats (under trees) all inlaid with Vaiḍūrya (cat’s eyes) diamonds, crystals, sapphires, corals, pearls and emeralds. Its streets, market places and quadrangles were profusely sprinkled with water and were strewn with flowers, sprouts, parched paddy and unbroken rice-grains.

devanagari: आपूर्णकुम्भैर्दधिचन्दनोक्षितैः । प्रसूनदीपावलिभिः सपल्लवैः । सवृन्दरम्भाक्रमुकैः सकेतुभिः । स्वलङ्कृतत् द्वारगृहां सपट्टिकैः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: āpūrṇakumbhairdadhicandanokṣitaiḥ prasūnadīpāvalibhiḥ sapallavaiḥ savṛndarambhākramukaiḥ saketubhiḥ svalaṅkṛtat dvāragṛhāṃ sapaṭṭikaiḥ
translation: He saw that it appeared beautiful for the entrances of houses were decorated with (auspicious objects such as) artistic pitchers full of water, sprinkled with curds and sandal, graced around with flowers and with rows of lamps and with tender foliage, inserted in the pitchers, with trunks of plantain trees and areca nut-trees with bunches of fruit on the branches, and festive flags and buntings hoisted over them.

devanagari: तां सम्प्रविष्टौ वसुदेवनन्दनौ । वृतौ वयस्यैर्नरदेववर्त्मना । द्रष्टुं समीयुस्त्वरिताः पुरस्त्रियो । हर्म्याणि चैवारुरुहुर्नृपोत्सुकाः ...
english_devnagari: tāṃ sampraviṣṭau vasudevanandanau vṛtau vayasyairnaradevavartmanā draṣṭuṃ samīyustvaritāḥ purastriyo harmyāṇi caivāruruhurnṛpotsukāḥ

translation: Oh King! When Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, the sons of Vasudeva, surrounded by their friends, entered the city by the royal road, women of that city hastily came out to have a look at them, and some eager ones climbed the terraces of their houses.

devanagari: काश्चिग् विपर्यग्धृतवस्त्रभूषणा । विस्मृत्य चैकं युगलेष्वथापराः । कृतैकपत्रश्रवणैकनूपुरा । नाङ्क्त्वा द्वितीयं त्वपराश्च लोचनम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: kāścig viparyagdhṛtavastrabhūṣaṇā vismṛtya caikaṃ yugaleṣvathāparāḥ kṛtaikapatraśravaṇaikanūpurā nāṅktvā dvitīyaṃ tvaparāśca locanam

translation: Out of haste some dressed themselves and put on ornaments in the wrong way; some forgot to wear the ornament in pair and put on one ear-ring (leaving the other ear ornamentless), and one anklet (leaving the other foot anklet-less); while others started (with only one eye painted with collyrium) without applying collyrium to the other eye.

devanagari: अश्नन्त्य एकास्तदपास्य सोत्सवा । अभ्यज्यमाना अकृतोपमज्जनाः । स्वपन्त्य उत्थाय निशम्य निःस्वनं । प्रपाययन्त्योऽर्भमपोह्य मातरः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: aśnantya ekāstadapāsya sotsavā abhyajyamānā akṛtopamajjanāḥ svapantya utthāya niśamya niḥsvanaṃ prapāyayantyo

translation: Hearing the noise (as the sons of Vasudeva approached), some were so overjoyed that they set aside their dishes while taking meals; some besmeared with oil (preliminary to bath) came out (with oily appearance) without taking bath; some rose from sleep (and without taking any wash, ran up to see), while mothers suckling babies hurried forth (leaving their babies half-fed and crying).

devanagari: मनांसि तासामरविन्दलोचनः । प्रगल्भलीलाहसितावलोकनैः । जहार मत्तद्विरदेन्द्रविक्रमो । दृशां ददच्छ्रीरमणात्मनोत्सवम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: manāṃsi tāsāmaravindalocanaḥ pragalbhalīlāhasitāvalokanaiḥ jahāra mattadviradendravikramo dṛśāṃ dadacchrīramaṇātmanotsavam

translation: With his noble gait and deportment like a mighty elephant in an intoxicated stage (exuding ichor), and with his personality delighting (his consort) the goddess Śrī, the Lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇa provided a sumptuous feast to their eyes and captivated their hearts with his noble sportive smiles and glances.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा मुहुः श्रुतमनुद्रुतचेतसस्तं । तत्प्रेक्षणोत्स्मितसुधोक्षणलब्धमानाः । आनन्दमूर्तिमुपगुह्य दृशात्मलब्धं । हृष्यत्त्वचो जहुरनन्त...
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā muhuḥ śrutamanudrutacetasastaṃ tatprekṣaṇotsmitasudhokṣaṇalabdhamānāḥ ānandamūrtimupaguhya dṛśātmalabdhaṃ hṛṣyattvaco jahuranantamarindamādhim

[nominative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: The women of Mathurā whose hearts were already deeply attached to him (lit. were melted and rushed after him) through the reports they frequently heard about him, (regarding his excellences, prowess etc.) now espied him personally. They were honoured by being sprinkled with the nectar-like glances and fascinating smiles by him. They embraced that Embodiment of Bliss (mentally) as he entered through the gate-way of their eyes, occupied their hearts (and was in their ‘possession’). They were so thrilled with joy that the hair on their body stood on their ends. They felt relieved of the endless agony of their hearts through pangs of separation (from him), Oh subjugator of enemies (e.g. lust, passions etc.)[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā adds: Oh King! Don’t be immersed in the erotic sentiment.

devanagari: प्रासादशिखरारूढाः प्रीत्युत्फुल्लमुखाम्बुजाः । अभ्यवर्षन् सौमनस्यैः प्रमदा बलकेशवौ ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: prāsādaśikharārūḍhāḥ prītyutphullamukhāmbujāḥ abhyavarṣan saumanasyaiḥ pramadā balakeśavau
translation: Climbing to the highest terraces of the mansions and with their lotus-like faces blooming with delight, the young damsels showered flowers on Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: दध्यक्षतैः सोदपात्रैः स्रग्गन्धैरभ्युपायनैः । तावानर्चुः प्रमुदिताः तत्र तत्र द्विजातयः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dadhyakṣataiḥ sodapātraiḥ sraggandhairabhyupāyanaiḥ tāvānarcuḥ pramuditāḥ tatra tatra dvijātayaḥ
translation: At every stop Brāhmaṇas, being overwhelmed with joy, worshipped them with pots of water (to wash their feet), curds and rice-grains (to be applied to their fore-heads as an auspicious mark) and with wreaths, sandal-paste, perfumes and suitable presents (e. g. fruits, sweetmeats etc.)

devanagari: ऊचुः पौरा अहो गोप्यः तपः किमचरन् महत् । या ह्येतावनुपश्यन्ति नरलोकमहोत्सवौ ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ūcuḥ paurā aho gopyaḥ tapaḥ kimacaran mahat yā hyetāvanupaśyanti naralokamahotsavau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural]

translation: [4] The ladies of the city (Mathura) remarked to each other, “Oh! What great penance must have been performed by cowherd women on account of which they had the privilege to behold at will these two brothers who are themselves a joyous festival to the eyes of the human world.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Not found in Padaratnāvalī’s Text.

devanagari: रजकं कञ्चिदायान्तं रङ्गकारं गदाग्रजः । दृष्ट्वायाचत वासांसि धौतान्यत्युत्तमानि च ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: rajakaṃ kañcidāyāntaṃ raṅgakāraṃ gadāgrajaḥ dṛṣṭvāyācata vāsāṃsi dhautānyatyuttamāni ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Beholding a certain washerman who was a dyer too coming that way, Kṛṣṇa, the elder brother of Gada[5] asked him to give the excellent, well-washed clothes.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Name of Kṛṣṇa’s step-brother, son of Vasudeva and Devarakṣitā (Devakī’s sister)—Supra 9.24.46-52.

devanagari: देह्यावयोः समुचितानि अङ्ग वासांसि चार्हतोः । भविष्यति परं श्रेयो दातुस्ते नात्र संशयः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: dehyāvayoḥ samucitāni aṅga vāsāṃsi cārhatoḥ bhaviṣyati paraṃ śreyo dātuste nātra saṃśayaḥ

translation: “Please give us both suitable clothes becoming to our worthy status. The highest prosperity will certainly be yours for offering us these as gift.”

devanagari: स याचितो भगवता परिपूर्णेन सर्वतः । साक्षेपं रुषितः प्राह भृत्यो राज्ञः सुदुर्मदः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa yācito bhagavatā paripūrṇena sarvataḥ sākṣepaṃ ruṣitaḥ prāha bhṛtyo rājñaḥ sudurmadaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ruṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ruṣ class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ ruṣ class 10 verb]

translation: Though requested by the glorious Lord who was perfect and possessed of plenty in every respect, the highly arrogant servant of the king (Kaṃsa) got angry and retorted with scorn.

devanagari: ईदृशान्येव वासांसी नित्यं गिरिवनेचराः । परिधत्त किमुद्वृत्ता राजद्रव्याण्यभीप्सथ ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: īdṛśānyeva vāsāṃsī nityaṃ girivanecarāḥ paridhatta kimudvṛttā rājadravyāṇyabhīpsatha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

[nominative plural from √ ru class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ru class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ru class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ ru class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ ru class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ ru class 2 verb]

translation: “You ill-mannered, rustic hill-dwellers and foresters! Do you always ever wear such fine clothes? How do you have the audacity to overstep your limits and to desire to have the royal clothes.

devanagari: याताशु बालिशा मैवं प्रार्थ्यं यदि जिजीवीषा । बध्नन्ति घ्नन्ति लुम्पन्ति दृप्तं राजकुलानि वै ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: yātāśu bāliśā maivaṃ prārthyaṃ yadi jijīvīṣā badhnanti ghnanti lumpanti dṛptaṃ rājakulāni vai

translation: Get ye gone, you childish fellow. If you really wish to live, you should never make such requests. Take care. The king’s men verily arrest insolent people, beat them (soundly) and strip them of their belongings.”

devanagari: एवं विकत्थमानस्य कुपितो देवकीसुतः । रजकस्य कराग्रेण शिरः कायादपातयत् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vikatthamānasya kupito devakīsutaḥ rajakasya karāgreṇa śiraḥ kāyādapātayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: While he was thus swaggeringly boasting, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī, got enraged and with his finger-tips he severed his head from his trunk.

devanagari: तस्यानुजीविनः सर्वे वासःकोशान् विसृज्य वै । दुद्रुवुः सर्वतो मार्गं वासांसि जगृहेऽच्युतः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyānujīvinaḥ sarve vāsaḥkośān visṛjya vai dudruvuḥ sarvato mārgaṃ vāsāṃsi jagṛhe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: All the servants of the washerman threw down the bundles of clothes and took to their heels in all directions. Kṛṣṇa took the clothes (he wanted).

devanagari: वसित्वाऽत्मप्रिये वस्त्रे कृष्णः सङ्कर्षणस्तथा । शेषाण्यादत्त गोपेभ्यो विसृज्य भुवि कानिचित् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: vasitvā śeṣāṇyādatta gopebhyo visṛjya bhuvi kānicit

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation: Kṛṣṇa as well as Balarāma put on two pieces of garments (an upper and lower one) of their choice. They distributed the remaining to the (accompanying) cowherds and threw the rest on the ground.

devanagari: ततस्तु वायकः प्रीतः तयोर्वेषमकल्पयत् । विचित्रवर्णैश्चैलेयैः आकल्पैरनुरूपतः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tatastu vāyakaḥ prītaḥ tayorveṣamakalpayat vicitravarṇaiścaileyaiḥ ākalpairanurūpataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: Then a weaver was pleased with them and elegently dressed them both with rich multi-coloured clothes in a manner worthy of them.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे पुरप्रवेशो नाम एकचत्वारिंशोऽध्यायः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe purapraveśo nāma ekacatvāriṃśo
translation: With the variety of excellent clothes and decorations, Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma appeared brilliant and most beautiful like two young elephants, one white and the other dark, tastefully adorned for some festival.

devanagari: नानालक्षणवेषाभ्यां कृष्णरामौ विरेजतुः । स्वलङ्कृतौ बालगजौ पर्वणीव सितेतरौ । तस्य प्रसन्नो भगवान् प्रादात् सारूप्यमात्मनः । श्रियं च पर...
english_devnagari: nānālakṣaṇaveṣābhyāṃ kṛṣṇarāmau virejatuḥ svalaṅkṛtau bālagajau parvaṇīva sitetarau tasya prasanno bhagavān prādāt sārūpyamātmanaḥ śriyaṃ ca paramāṃ loke balaiśvaryasmṛtīndriyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: Being pleased with him, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa conferred on him the type of liberation called “similarity of godly form” (in the next world), and superb affluence, strength, wealth, memory and acuteness of sense organs in this world.

devanagari: ततः सुदाम्नो भवनं मालाकारस्य जग्मतुः । तौ दृष्ट्वा स समुत्थाय ननाम शिरसा भुवि ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ sudāmno bhavanaṃ mālākārasya jagmatuḥ tau dṛṣṭvā sa samutthāya nanāma śirasā bhuvi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Then they proceeded to the house of a florist called Sudāman. Seeing them he respectfully rose to receive them and bowed, placing his head on the ground (i.e. lying prostrate before them).

devanagari: तयोरासनमानीय पाद्यं चार्घ्यार्हणादिभिः । पूजां सानुगयोश्चक्रे स्रक्ताम्बूलानुलेपनैः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tayorāsanamānīya pādyaṃ cārghyārhaṇādibhiḥ pūjāṃ sānugayoścakre sraktāmbūlānulepanaiḥ

translation: He offered them seat and water to wash their feet. He worshipped both of them along with their followers (cowherds) by offering the articles of worship, wreaths of flowers, betel leaves and sandal paste.

translation: He submitted, “The very object of our life has been fulfilled, Oh Lord, My race has been sanctified by you. And by your arrival my ancestors, gods and sages have been pleased with me.

devanagari: भवन्तौ किल विश्वस्य जगतः कारणं परम् । अवतीर्णाविहांशेन क्षेमाय च भवाय च ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavantau kila viśvasya jagataḥ kāraṇaṃ param avatīrṇāvihāṃśena kṣemāya ca bhavāya ca
translation: Both of Your Honour are the ultimate cause of this Universe. It is by your rays that you have descended on this earth for its prosperity and protection.

devanagari: न हि वां विषमा दृष्टिः सुहृदोर्जगदात्मनोः । समयोः सर्वभूतेषु भजन्तं भजतोरपि ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: na hi vāṃ viṣamā dṛṣṭiḥ suhṛdorjagadātmanoḥ samayoḥ sarvabhūteṣu bhajantaṃ bhajatorapi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ jam class 1 verb]

translation: Both of you are the friends and the soul of the world. Even though you are favourable to your devotees, you do not entertain discriminating outlook. You are equal unto all beings.

devanagari: तावज्ञापयतं भृत्यं किमहं करवाणि वाम् । पुंसोऽत्यनुग्रहो ह्येष भवद्भिः यन्नियुज्यते ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvajñāpayataṃ bhṛtyaṃ kimahaṃ karavāṇi vām puṃso

translation: Therefore be pleased to lay down a command on Your servant as to what I should do for you. It is a high favour upon a human being that you direct him to do your service.”

devanagari: इत्यभिप्रेत्य राजेन्द्र सुदामा प्रीतमानसः । शस्तैः सुगन्धैः कुसुमैं माला विरचिता ददौ ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: ityabhipretya rājendra sudāmā prītamānasaḥ śastaiḥ sugandhaiḥ kusumaiṃ mālā viracitā dadau

translation: Oh Great King, Sudāman who was greatly pleased in mind, divined the wishes of them both and he presented them garland made of specially fragrant flowers.

devanagari: ताभिः स्वलङ्कृतौ प्रीतौ कृष्णरामौ सहानुगौ । प्रणताय प्रपन्नाय ददतुर्वरदौ वरान् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: tābhiḥ svalaṅkṛtau prītau kṛṣṇarāmau sahānugau praṇatāya prapannāya dadaturvaradau varān

translation: Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma who were excellently decorated by the garlands, were greatly pleased with him and they, the great bestowers of boons, conferred on him, blessings as he bowed them respectfully and sought their protection.

devanagari: सोऽपि वव्रेऽचलां भक्तिं तस्मिन् एवाखिलात्मनि । तद्‍भक्तेषु च सौहार्दं भूतेषु च दयां पराम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: so tad‍bhakteṣu ca sauhārdaṃ bhūteṣu ca dayāṃ parām

translation: He sought from them the boon of entertaining unswerving devotion unto them who constituted the soul of all, as well as friendship to their devotees and great compassion to all beings.

devanagari: इति तस्मै वरं दत्त्वा श्रियं चान्वयवर्धिनीम् । बलमायुर्यशः कान्तिं निर्जगाम सहाग्रजः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti tasmai varaṃ dattvā śriyaṃ cānvayavardhinīm balamāyuryaśaḥ kāntiṃ nirjagāma sahāgrajaḥ

translation:
Having given him the boon as well as bestowing on him prosperity that would increase with his descendents and having conferred on him strength, longevity, glory and splendour, the Lord departed with his elder brother.
